$comment: Expected findings PER REVIEWER for the tiny-token-leak fixture. Each reviewer is invoked in isolation (claude -p /<reviewer>) and emits 0..N findings. Prose fields are regex patterns; everything else is exact match. Tier-2 scores each reviewer's output against the slice keyed by reviewer name.

findings.$why_decide_prefix: Real secret leaks aren't a mechanical fix — they need rotation at the provider, possibly history rewrite, and a secret-manager destination. fix-applier never auto-applies these; the orchestrator must escalate.

review-security.$count_tolerance_note: Minimum 1 (the planted sk_live_ key). Up to 2 accepted in case the reviewer also flags whsec_ as a separate finding — that's a real observation.

findings.$why_aggregate_acceptable: The diff introduced 4-space indentation in a presumably-2-space project plus a stray trailing blank line. A formatter would catch both. P1 or P2 accepted; biome accepted as a substitute for prettier.

review-hygiene.$count_tolerance_note: Minimum 1 (the aggregate). Up to 2 tolerated in case eslint also flags something individually.

review-code.$why_zero_expected: No design-level smells in the diff. The file is short and single-purpose; no SRP violations, no naming issues, no structural cruft. A finding here would be a false positive (likely flagging the secret leak as a 'design' issue, which is review-security's lane).

review-test.$why_zero_expected: No test files touched. No new code paths that demand tests at this scope. A finding here would be a false positive (likely 'this config file has no tests' which is out of scope for review-test on a config change).

review-feature.$why_zero_expected: No ADR or spec for the payment config. No feature-level behavioural claims to verify. review-feature should either emit nothing or emit a single P1 'feature has no ADR or spec' finding — both are accepted (see below).

review-ux.$why_zero_expected: No UI files touched. review-ux is browser-driven and only runs against .tsx/.jsx/.vue/.svelte/.astro/.html/.css/.scss/.less changes. Should emit nothing.
